explainability.path_to_improvement: Document how explanations are provided to users affected by AI decisions.

external_accountability.path_to_improvement: Obtain external validation of AI practices or require vendor certifications.

fairness.path_to_improvement: Document bias testing procedures or vendor AI fairness requirements.

governance.findings: Textron's 2025 10-K references various model-based valuation methods, including discounted cash flow and matrix pricing, and the adoption of new systems. A CIO.com article discusses navigating risk and deployment, mentioning guardrails and a governance approach.
governance.path_to_improvement: Name an AI governance body with defined mandate covering all AI use.

relevant_sources.summary: This SEC filing, Textron's 2025 10-K, provides evidence for the **governance** and **transparency** pillars of responsible AI. The filing supports governance by referencing various model-based valuation methods, including discounted cash flow and matrix pricing, as well as the adoption of new systems. Transparency is supported by the mention of "Autoland technology."
relevant_sources.title: Form 10-K

relevant_sources.summary: The CIO.com article "Textron Takes Flight with Gen AI" provides evidence for the **governance** and **transparency** pillars of responsible AI. The article mentions Textron's proprietary Gen AI solution, TAMI, and its use of RAG for mechanics, implying capabilities that support transparency. Furthermore, the discussion of navigating risk and deployment, along with the mention of guardrails and a governance approach, offers some support for the governance pillar, though specific mechanisms are not detailed.
relevant_sources.title: Textron Takes Flight with Gen AI (CIO.com)

oversight.path_to_improvement: Document human review processes for AI-assisted decisions (built or vendor AI).

privacy.path_to_improvement: Document data protection practices for AI systems, including vendor AI data handling.

transparency.findings: Textron's 2025 10-K references Autoland technology. A CIO.com article mentions Textron's proprietary Gen AI solution, TAMI, and its use of RAG for mechanics.
transparency.path_to_improvement: Publish detailed documentation (model cards, system specs) for AI systems deployed.

relevant_sources.summary: This SEC filing, Textron's 2025 10-K, provides evidence for the **governance** and **transparency** pillars of responsible AI. The filing supports governance by referencing various model-based valuation methods, including discounted cash flow and matrix pricing, as well as the adoption of new systems. Transparency is supported by the mention of "Autoland technology."
relevant_sources.title: Form 10-K

relevant_sources.summary: The CIO.com article "Textron Takes Flight with Gen AI" provides evidence for the **governance** and **transparency** pillars of responsible AI. The article mentions Textron's proprietary Gen AI solution, TAMI, and its use of RAG for mechanics, implying capabilities that support transparency. Furthermore, the discussion of navigating risk and deployment, along with the mention of guardrails and a governance approach, offers some support for the governance pillar, though specific mechanisms are not detailed.

summary.overall_findings: Drawing from 13 publicly available sources, Textron's published materials address transparency and governance, representing 2 of 7 evaluated responsible AI pillars. Transparency disclosures reference Textron's proprietary Gen AI solution, TAMI, and Textron's 2025 10-K also references Autoland technology. For governance, the 2025 10-K notes the adoption of new systems and various model-based valuation methods, including discounted cash flow. No qualifying public evidence was found for the majority of the 7 evaluated pillars.
